faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal absent from Taxi-to-Driver API (3.2.2). Both UC 1.1 Task 4 and UC 1.5 Task 4 require the central to confirm an order to the driver, but the taxi module has no Confirm signal in 3.2.2 to forward this confirmation from the central (3.4.1 Confirm) to the driver.

No signal from Central to Taxi to inform the driver that no taxis are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires the driver to be notified so a new order with a different pick-up time can be submitted, but no such signal is defined in the Central-to-Taxi API.

Order_struct contains no priority field. Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized and some customers are more important than others, but the data structure defines no attribute to represent this priority.

No signal for sending a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires the system to present how long it takes for a car to be available, but no corresponding Central-to-Operator signal is defined in 3.3.2.

LoginSignal is defined without parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send driver identification information and position to the central at login, but LoginSignal carries no data fields and therefore cannot convey any driver ID or position.

LoggedIn(integer) carries only the taxi number. Task 3 requires driver information to be transmitted to the central upon login, but the LoggedIn signal has no driver identification parameter, making it impossible to associate the login event with a specific driver.

faults.use_case: 1.4 Taxi: Log in / 1.9 Taxi: View traffic overview
faults.description: ZoneInfo signal is absent from the Central-to-Taxi API (3.4.1). UC 1.4 Task 6 requires the central to start sending zone overview information to the car after login; UC 1.9 Task 2 requires it in response to an Overview request from the driver. Neither can be fulfilled without a ZoneInfo signal in 3.4.1.

LoginFailed signal absent from Central-to-Taxi API (3.4.1). Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid driver card and keep the car in Offline state, but no LoginFailed signal exists in 3.4.1 to communicate the rejection to the taxi module.

No signal from Central to Taxi for automatic order cancellation after the 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires the order to be automatically canceled if neither accepted nor rejected within 2 minutes, but no cancellation notification signal is defined in 3.4.1.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal to communicate the Waiting-for-Customer state to the central. After accepting an order (UC 1.5 Task 6), the car must enter Waiting-for-Customer state before meter start, but no signal for this state transition is defined in the Taxi-to-Central API.

MSC 4.1 is missing a Central-to-Taxi Confirm step after step 8 (Ack received by central). UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order to the driver, but no Confirm signal from Central to Taxi is shown in the MSC after the Ack.

Position(integer, integer) carries only taxi number and zone number but no time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send both arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central when entering Soon-Available state, but the signal cannot convey the time component.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal defined in the Taxi-to-Central API. UC 1.8 Tasks 1-2 require the taxi to immediately alert the central and open a voice channel when the driver activates the alarm, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

Position(integer, integer) transmits zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires the taxi to send exact coordinates to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm event, but the signal provides only a coarse zone identifier.

No Overview request signal defined in the Taxi-to-Central API. Task 1 requires the driver to request traffic overview from the terminal, which the taxi module must forward to the central, but no such signal is defined in 3.4.2. Table 1 encodes an OverviewSignal (Message Type Id 9) for the radio link, indicating the signal was intended but omitted from the API definition.

No signal to notify the operator when a designated car rejects a manually dispatched order. Variant 4b requires the operator to be informed so the order can be reassigned to another car, but no rejection notification signal is defined in the Central-to-Operator API.

Text_Message(integer, integer, charstring) in 3.3.1 provides no group mechanism. Task 3 requires the operator to send to a specific car, a group of cars, or all cars. The signal supports only a specific taxi number or all taxis (parameter 0), but no group of cars can be addressed.

No text message acknowledgment signal in the Taxi-to-Central API. Task 4 requires each car receiving a text message to automatically acknowledge receipt to the central, but no acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2.

No signal to notify the operator of failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b explicitly requires this notification, but no corresponding signal exists in the Central-to-Operator API.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal defined in the Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 Tasks 1-3 require operators to authenticate with user identification and password, but 3.3.1 defines no signal for submitting login credentials to the system.

Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is placed in 3.3.1 (Operator-to-Central signals) but its description reads 'this is the response', indicating it is a system response to the operator's query. A response signal belongs in 3.3.2 (Central-to-Operator signals), not in 3.3.1.

Voice_msg in 3.4.2 is defined with 2 parameters (taxi number, voice data), but MSC 4.2 step 16 shows Taxi sending Voice_Msg(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data) with 3 parameters. The operator number is missing from the Taxi-to-Central Voice_msg signal definition.

Confirm_Voice is missing from the User-to-Taxi signals (3.2.1). MSC 4.2 step 5 shows the Driver sending Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) to the Taxi module to confirm voice connection setup, but this signal is not defined in 3.2.1.

No signal for the driver to request car and driver properties (missing from 3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver the requested information (missing from 3.4.1). UC 1.16 Tasks 1-2 require both a request and a response signal, but neither is defined.

faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal (Central-to-Taxi order confirmation, defined in 3.4.1) is not present in the communication protocol Table 1. Since Confirm is transmitted over the radio link, it must be encoded in Table 1, but no Confirm message type exists there.

No signal for updating driver properties in the Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 Tasks 2-3 require the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no signal to support this interaction.
